name: KWDEV Prompt Engineering Checklist

guidance.7_8: Strong baseline with clear improvements remaining

categories.title: Output Contract Design
checks: Every prompt has explicit output mode (json_only or markdown_doc_only)
Expected JSON top-level shape is explicit (object vs array)
Output examples are concise and aligned with parser expectations

categories.title: Parser Reliability and Recovery
checks: Strict parser rejects mixed prose + JSON output
Retry path exists with stronger output reminder
Error responses surface useful raw snippet for debugging

categories.title: Prompt Injection and Input Safety
checks: Untrusted content is wrapped in explicit begin/end boundaries
Prompt text says enclosed content is data, not instructions
Sensitive flows that combine file content and instructions are guarded

categories.title: App-Purpose Alignment (Execution-First)
checks: Execution paths prioritize concrete actions and verification
ask/plan/agent modes are semantically consistent in metadata and prompt text
Prompt guidance matches agent runtime behavior

categories.title: Prompt Source of Truth and Drift Control
checks: Prompt file pairs are validated (.prompt.md and .prompt.json)
Drift detection exists between markdown and source_markdown payload
Canonical authoring/source policy is explicit and enforced

categories.title: Reuse and Composability
checks: Shared prompt helper utilities are used across routes
Cross-route duplication of guardrails is minimized
Prompt builders are modular for context blocks

categories.title: Validation and Type Safety
checks: Request schemas validate input bounds and required fields
Post-parse normalization includes allowlists and limits
Core contracts are represented with clear TypeScript types

categories.title: Observability and Failure Diagnostics
checks: Failures are returned with actionable messages
Critical flows include useful operational logs
Error categories are distinguishable (validation vs parse vs runtime)

categories.title: Testing and Governance
checks: Contract helpers are unit tested
Route-level hardening checks exist for key endpoints
Prompt catalog/governance checks cover drift and structure

best_practices: Use explicit output contracts with strict parsers
Treat all user text and document content as untrusted input
Prefer shared helpers over route-local ad hoc regex parsing
Keep one canonical source for prompt authoring, validate derived artifacts
Align UI intent, runtime metadata, and prompt wording
Cap lengths and enforce enum allowlists before persistence
Fail loudly with actionable diagnostics and short raw snippets
Back all hardening changes with focused tests
